title: Compose a Ten from Ones
description: Recognizes that 10 ones can be regrouped as 1 ten when determining a standard two-digit number.
condition: A representation includes 10 or more ones (e.g., 5 tens and 11 ones).
response: Convert each group of 10 ones into 1 additional ten and adjust the total.
label: Principle / Model
example: 5 tens and 11 ones = 6 tens and 1 one = $61$.

title: Count Forward or Backward by Ones
description: Identifies numbers that come immediately before or after a given number when counting by ones.
condition: Prompt asks for numbers before or after a given number in the counting sequence (e.g., before/after 80).
response: State the next two numbers (+1, +2) or previous two numbers (−1, −2).
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Order of listing before numbers may vary (78, 79) depending on prompt interpretation.

title: Decompose Two-Digit Number into Tens and Ones
description: Breaks a two-digit number into its tens and ones components.
response: Express the number as tens + ones (e.g., 50 + 3).
label: Principle / Model

title: Interpret Base-Ten Representation
description: Determines the two-digit number represented by tens rods and ones cubes or verbal statements of tens and ones.
condition: A representation shows a number using tens and ones (pictures, words, or expanded form).
response: Combine tens and ones to state the standard two-digit number.
label: Principle / Model

title: Subtract a Multiple of Ten
description: Subtracts a multiple of ten from a two-digit number by decreasing the tens digit while keeping the ones digit the same.
condition: Expression of the form a two-digit number − multiple of 10 (e.g., 60 − 20).
response: Decrease the tens digit by the number of tens subtracted and keep the ones digit unchanged.
label: Principle / Model
example: $60 - 20 = 40$ because 6 tens minus 2 tens equals 4 tens.
